title: Back references in regular expressions should only refer to capturing groups that are matched before the reference
description: This rule checks if a regular expression contains a backreference that refers to a capturing group that has not been matched yet.
message: Backreference refers to unmatched capturing group
recommendation: Reorder the capturing groups or adjust the backreference accordingly

noncompliant.code:
import re
pattern = r'(?P<group1>a)(?P<group2>b)\k<group1>
match = re.search(pattern, 'abbb')
noncompliant.description: Backreference refers to a capturing group that has not been matched yet

compliant.code:
import re
pattern = r'(?P<group1>a)(?P<group2>b)(?P=group1)
match = re.search(pattern, 'ab')
compliant.description: Backreference refers to a capturing group that has already been matched

compliant.code:
import re
pattern = r'(?P<group1>a)(?P=group1)(?P<group2>b)
match = re.search(pattern, 'aaab')
compliant.description: Backreference refers to a capturing group that has already been matched

why_is_this_an_issue: Backreferences should only refer to capturing groups that have already been matched to ensure the regular expression behaves as intended.
how_to_fix: Adjust the order of capturing groups or the backreference to ensure it refers to a capturing group that has already been matched.

checks.message: Backreference refers to unmatched capturing group (numbered)
Backreference refers to unmatched capturing group (named)
